steps.title: Identify the cause-and-effect structure
steps.content: The passage establishes that air is owned and sold by companies, then the blank precedes a statement about what people must do. The transition must connect the setup to what logically follows from it.

steps.title: Apply the consequence transition
steps.content:
”As a result” directly expresses that purchasing air is the logical consequence of companies owning and selling it.

The other choices fail to show this cause-effect relationship: “Nevertheless” and “In any case” imply contradiction or reluctant acceptance despite obstacles, while “Earlier” introduces temporal reference that doesn't apply.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
